expectations.text: Report evaluates visual appearance (layout, colors, typography)
expectations.evidence: Phase 1 covers layout & spacing, colors & contrast, typography with specific values

expectations.text: Report traces the user journey (intro → form pages → review → confirmation)
expectations.evidence: Phase 2 traces complete flow: intro page → form sections → review accordions → submission

expectations.text: Report checks Section 508 / WCAG 2.1 AA compliance
expectations.evidence: Phase 3 dedicated to WCAG 2.1 AA with specific criteria citations

expectations.text: Report checks screen reader compatibility (ARIA, labels, heading hierarchy)
expectations.evidence: Checks heading hierarchy, label associations, aria-expanded, aria-live regions

expectations.text: Report checks keyboard navigation feasibility
expectations.evidence: Evaluates tab order, focus indicators, keyboard accessibility of all interactive elements

expectations.text: Report includes a test verdict (pass/conditional pass/fail)
expectations.evidence: Verdict: CONDITIONAL PASS — accessibility violations must be fixed before publication

expectations.text: Report explains issues in user-impact terms, not just code terms
expectations.evidence: Frames issues as 'breaks screen reader flow', 'Veteran filling out the form' perspective throughout

expectations.text: Report evaluates visual appearance (layout, colors, typography)
expectations.evidence: Section 1 covers visual design with color, typography, and layout notes

expectations.text: Report traces the user journey (intro → form pages → review → confirmation)
expectations.evidence: Only tests Step 1 to Step 2 navigation; does not trace full journey through review and confirmation

expectations.text: Report checks Section 508 / WCAG 2.1 AA compliance
expectations.evidence: Mentions accessibility concerns but does not perform structured 508/WCAG audit

expectations.text: Report checks screen reader compatibility (ARIA, labels, heading hierarchy)
expectations.evidence: Notes 'Could not verify if all form inputs have proper aria-label' — does not actually check

expectations.text: Report checks keyboard navigation feasibility
expectations.evidence: Recommends keyboard testing but does not evaluate tab order or focus management

expectations.text: Report includes a test verdict (pass/conditional pass/fail)
expectations.evidence: Overall Assessment: FUNCTIONAL with minor issues

expectations.text: Report explains issues in user-impact terms, not just code terms
expectations.evidence: Written from Veteran user perspective with practical impact descriptions

expectations.text: Report focuses primarily on accessibility rather than visual design
expectations.evidence: Phase 3 (accessibility) is the largest section; visual is brief and supportive

expectations.text: Report checks heading hierarchy for screen readers
expectations.evidence: Verifies H1 > H2 > H3 hierarchy with specific elements cited

expectations.text: Report checks form field label associations
expectations.evidence: Confirms for/id label pairing pattern across all field types

expectations.text: Report checks ARIA attributes on dynamic elements (accordions, alerts)
expectations.evidence: Checks aria-expanded on accordions, notes missing aria-live for dynamic content

expectations.text: Report checks color contrast ratios
expectations.evidence: Specific ratios: primary blue 8.6:1, body text 15.4:1, error 5.2:1, success green 4.58:1

expectations.text: Report references specific WCAG criteria by number
expectations.evidence: References 1.3.1, 2.4.6, 4.1.2, 1.4.3, and other specific criteria

expectations.text: Report focuses primarily on accessibility rather than visual design
expectations.evidence: Report is structured as an accessibility audit with 508 focus

expectations.text: Report checks heading hierarchy for screen readers
expectations.evidence: Section 3: confirms H1/H2/H3 hierarchy, WCAG 1.3.1

expectations.text: Report checks form field label associations
expectations.evidence: Section 5: verifies for/id label association with code examples

expectations.text: Report checks ARIA attributes on dynamic elements (accordions, alerts)
expectations.evidence: Does not specifically check accordion aria-expanded or alert role attributes

expectations.text: Report checks color contrast ratios
expectations.evidence: Does not calculate or cite specific contrast ratios

expectations.text: Report references specific WCAG criteria by number
expectations.evidence: References 1.3.1, 3.1.1, 1.1.1, 2.1.1, 2.4.6 throughout

expectations.text: Report confirms green action link IS correct per BIO VaLinkAction pattern
expectations.evidence: Assessment: CORRECT — 'Uses the proper play triangle character, matches VA.gov BIO pattern exactly'

expectations.text: Report confirms U+203A on Continue button IS correct per BIO pattern
expectations.evidence: Assessment: CORRECT — identifies U+203A as correct per BIO specification

expectations.text: Report confirms U+2039 on Back button IS correct per BIO pattern
expectations.evidence: Assessment: CORRECT — confirms \u2039 left guillemet matches BIO pattern

expectations.text: Report explains WHY these patterns are correct (references VA.gov source)
expectations.evidence: References VA.gov BIO patterns, VaLinkAction component, and VADS design system as authority

expectations.text: Report evaluates whether these elements work well from a user perspective
expectations.evidence: Evaluates visual proportions, user perception, and notes these are established VA.gov patterns users expect

expectations.text: Report confirms green action link IS correct per BIO VaLinkAction pattern
expectations.evidence: INCORRECTLY flags triangle as 'undersized' and claims visual imbalance — does not recognize BIO pattern

expectations.text: Report confirms U+203A on Continue button IS correct per BIO pattern
expectations.evidence: Identifies character but claims 'style mismatch' between button types — misses that this IS the BIO pattern

expectations.text: Report confirms U+2039 on Back button IS correct per BIO pattern
expectations.evidence: Does not separately validate Back button arrow as correct

expectations.text: Report explains WHY these patterns are correct (references VA.gov source)
expectations.evidence: Has no BIO reference knowledge — treats patterns as potential issues rather than correct implementations

expectations.text: Report evaluates whether these elements work well from a user perspective
expectations.evidence: Does evaluate from user perspective, noting visual proportions and browser rendering considerations

notes: With-skill achieves 100% pass rate vs 48% without — strongest skill lift of both skills
Button Appearance Test shows the most dramatic difference: 100% vs 20% — without BIO reference, agent flags correct patterns as bugs
Without-skill Full Test used 55 tool calls (browser testing) vs 37 with skill — more tools used but less accurate
Accessibility Audit: without-skill misses contrast ratio calculations and accordion ARIA checks
Skill provides critical domain knowledge (BIO patterns) that prevents false positives on correct implementations
